children.heading: Terms and conditions for lands withdrawn or acquired for Department of the Interior
children.label: a Terms and conditions for lands withdrawn or acquired for Department of the Interior
children.text: Geothermal leases for lands withdrawn or acquired in aid of functions of the Department of the Interior may be issued only under such terms and conditions as the Secretary may prescribe to insure adequate utilization of the lands for the purposes for which they were withdrawn or acquired.

children.heading: Consent and terms and conditions for lands withdrawn or acquired for Department of Agriculture or for lands for power and related purposes
children.label: b Consent and terms and conditions for lands withdrawn or acquired for Department of Agriculture or for lands for power and related purposes
children.text: Geothermal leases for lands withdrawn or acquired in aid of functions of the Department of Agriculture may be issued only with the consent of, and subject to such terms and conditions as may be prescribed by, the head of that Department to insure adequate utilization of the lands for the purposes for which they were withdrawn or acquired. Geothermal leases for lands to which is applicable, may be issued only with the consent of, and subject to, such terms and conditions as the Secretary of Energy may prescribe to insure adequate utilization of such lands for power and related purposes.section 818 of title 16

children.heading: Exemption of certain Federal lands
children.label: c Exemption of certain Federal lands
children.text: Geothermal leases under this chapter shall not be issued for lands administered in accordance with (1) the Act of (), as amended or supplemented, (2) for lands within a national recreation area, (3) for lands in a fish hatchery administered by the Secretary, wildlife refuge, wildlife range, game range, wildlife management area, waterfowl production area, or for lands acquired or reserved for the protection and conservation of fish and wildlife that are threatened with extinction, (4) for tribally or individually owned Indian trust or restricted lands, within or without the boundaries of Indian reservations.August 25, 191639 Stat. 5351See References in Text note below.1
